title: Team Collaboration, Review, Governance, and Decision Handoffs · Product Scenario Checkpoint
summary: A review board compares near-miss decisions for team collaboration, review, governance, and decision handoffs and accepts only observable behavior, human evidence, explicit non-claims, and owned transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for collaboration operating system and review evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Decompose work into outcome-bearing, low-coupling slices with owner, interface, acceptance, dependencies, and integration point.
Link problem evidence, issue, decision, change, review, verification, release, and follow-up through stable identifiers.
Request review by risk area and require behavior, accessibility, security, data, operations, test, and documentation evidence.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# personal-project-2 cumulative independent product evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic evidence-contract review only.
# They never contact participants or services, execute commands, deploy, or claim live conformance.
workspace: portfolio
project-id: personal-project-2
stakeholder: [replace with affected people and accountable decision owner]
decision: [replace with the decision this evidence changes]
problem-evidence: [replace with observed need, counterevidence, and uncertainty]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
security: [replace with threat, authorization, abuse, response, and residual risk]
privacy: [replace with purpose, minimization, retention, access, correction, and deletion]
test-strategy: [replace with behavior, changed case, fault, accessibility, security, and recovery]
release-evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, inventory, provenance, validation, and rollback]
recovery: [replace with backup, clean restore, reconciliation, communication, and ownership]
portfolio-proof: [replace with contribution, limitations, counterexample, and unseen defense]
transfer-boundary: authorized controlled research, toolchain, deployment, and review environments
evidence:

steps.title: Work Decomposition · changed case · Quiz Work Decomposition
steps.instruction:
quiz case — Parallel contributors create oversized changes, invisible decisions, ambiguous ownership, rubber-stamp review, and a release nobody can fully explain. The quiz variation 5 changes the stakeholder, access need, evidence source, constraint, failure, and operating environment. Competency probe: Decompose work into outcome-bearing, low-coupling slices with owner, interface, acceptance, dependencies, and integration point.

Which action demonstrates Work Decomposition without dropping earlier contracts?

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Governance Health

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Maintain contribution, conduct, security, support, ownership, license, roadmap, release, and archival practices for project health.
